questions.question: Explain the universal law of gravitation and write its formula.
questions.answer: The universal law of gravitation states that every object in the universe attracts every other object with a force proportional to the product of their masses and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them. The formula is F = G(Mm)/d².

questions.question: Explain the factors affecting gravitational force between two objects.
questions.answer: Gravitational force depends on the masses of the objects and the distance between them. Larger masses increase the force, while greater distance decreases it.

questions.question: Describe the concept of free fall with suitable examples.
questions.answer: Free fall is the motion of an object under the influence of gravity alone. For example, a stone dropped from a height falls freely toward Earth.

questions.question: Explain acceleration due to gravity and factors affecting it.
questions.answer: Acceleration due to gravity is the acceleration gained by an object due to Earth's gravitational force. It depends on the mass and radius of Earth.
answer_outline: Dependence on Earth mass and radius

questions.question: Explain the difference between mass and weight with examples.
questions.answer: Mass is the amount of matter in an object and is constant everywhere. Weight is the gravitational force acting on an object and varies depending on gravity.

questions.question: Explain why the value of weight changes at different places.
questions.answer: Weight depends on gravitational acceleration which varies with location such as altitude, latitude and distance from Earth's center.

questions.question: Explain thrust and pressure with examples.
questions.answer: Thrust is the force acting perpendicular to a surface. Pressure is the thrust acting per unit area.

questions.question: Explain how pressure depends on area.
questions.answer: Pressure is inversely proportional to the area on which force acts. Smaller area produces greater pressure.

questions.question: Explain buoyant force and give examples.
questions.answer: Buoyant force is the upward force exerted by a fluid on an object immersed in it.

questions.question: Explain why objects float in water.
questions.answer: Objects float when the buoyant force acting on them is greater than or equal to their weight.

questions.question: Explain the concept of density in relation to floating.
questions.answer: Objects float if their density is less than that of the fluid and sink if their density is greater.

questions.question: State and explain Archimedes' principle.
questions.answer: Archimedes' principle states that a body immersed in fluid experiences an upward force equal to the weight of the fluid displaced.

questions.question: Explain the importance of Archimedes' principle.
questions.answer: It explains floating of ships, functioning of hydrometers and density measurement of liquids.

questions.question: Explain why ships float but nails sink.
questions.answer: Ships float because they displace a large volume of water producing sufficient buoyant force.

questions.question: Explain apparent weight in liquids.
questions.answer: Apparent weight is the reduced weight of an object when immersed in fluid due to buoyant force.

questions.question: Explain why dams are thicker at the bottom.
questions.answer: Water pressure increases with depth so dams are built thicker at the bottom to withstand greater pressure.

questions.question: Explain the principle behind hydrometers.
questions.answer: Hydrometers work based on Archimedes' principle to measure density of liquids.

questions.question: Explain why astronauts experience weightlessness.
questions.answer: Astronauts are in continuous free fall while orbiting Earth which creates a sensation of weightlessness.

questions.question: Explain the role of gravity in planetary motion.
questions.answer: Gravitational force between the Sun and planets keeps the planets in their orbits.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between gravitational force and distance.
questions.answer: Gravitational force decreases as the square of the distance between objects increases.

questions.question: Explain how submarines float and sink.
questions.answer: Submarines control buoyancy by adjusting ballast tanks to change density.

questions.question: Explain why camels walk easily on sand.
questions.answer: Their broad feet increase surface area and reduce pressure.

questions.question: Explain why wide tyres are used in heavy vehicles.
questions.answer: Wide tyres increase surface area reducing pressure on the ground.

questions.question: Explain the importance of gravity in daily life.
questions.answer: Gravity keeps objects on Earth, controls tides and maintains the atmosphere.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between density and buoyancy.
questions.answer: The buoyant force depends on the density of the fluid and the volume of fluid displaced.
